spans.text: Of what is great , or beautiful , or good , In Nature's general plan
what powers Of mortal art the sweet enchantment wrought
animate at will , The mimic scenes
Let us , subdued , Now to the magic of the moment lose The thoughts of life , and mingle every sense Even in the scenes before us !
up An incensed exhalation , like the meek And holy praise of Him whose soul's deep joy The lone woods witness
An incensed exhalation , like the meek And holy praise of Him whose soul's deep joy The lone woods witness
beneath , the bright blue champaign steams ; The banks , the meadows , and the flowers , send
of Him whose soul's deep joy The lone woods witness
the toiling crowd , still murmuring on , Restless

of Him Who , when the morning stars shouted for joy , Bade the great sun from tenfold darkness burst , The green earth roll in light , and solitude First hear the voice of man , while hills and woods Stood eminent , in orient hues arrayed
Pass the footbridge , and listen ( for we hear , Or think we hear her ) , listen to the song Of yonder milkmaid , as she brims her pail
the adverse bank , while , with firm hand
nearer yet , and yet more near
that in this hollow world Such bliss should be its portion
that in this hollow world Such bliss should be its portion
the innocent And feathered tenants of the landscape low
no spectre shape ( though most retired The spot ) there ever wandered , stoled in white , Along the midnight chambers ; but quaint Mab Her tiny revels led , till the rare dawn
chanticleer his shrill alarm Beneath the window rang
To him who owns the fair domain
of heartfelt joy Which scenes like these bestow , that charm the sight , Yet sooth the spirit
The fisher with his angle leans intent
! O , happiest he Who steals through life , untroubled as unseen
he Who steals through life , untroubled as unseen
No thought but that of pleasure more composed , As the winds whisper him to sounder sleep

The green leaves of his old paternal trees Make music , soothing as they stir : the elm , And poplar with its silvery trunk , that shades The green sward of the bank before his porch , Are to him as companions
The green leaves of his old paternal trees
the music of the selfsame trees Waving
that swells With honest English feelings , — while the eye , Saddened , but not cast down , beholds far off The darkness of the onward rolling storm
energy , the brave arm strength , England
more , My country , thy green nooks and inmost vales
the assemblage of the finished piece
gloom the sudden flash Of broken light upon the brawling stream Is flung below
The grey fane peering over the summer woods
the hollow road at eventide , Or browse the straggling branches
every light And every shade , greatly opposed , and all Subserving to one magical effect Of truth and harmony
Enamoured of the varied imagery , That fills the vivid canvas , swelling still On the enraptured eye of taste
On the enraptured eye of taste
the beauties of the pastoral bard
this pomp of light and shade Wake thee
this pomp of light and shade
we trace This summer-mantling view ) hoar Ætna's pines , The vine-hung grotts
